chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special Customs Reference Application No. 02 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No. 2175/25. 2. For hearing of main case. 18.11.2025 Agha Shahid Majeed Khan, advocate for applicant. On 18.04.2025, following order was passed:- “18.04.2025. Mr. Agha Shahid Majeed Khan, advocate for applicant. Weeks’ time allowed for compliance.” On 14.10.2025, following order was passed:- “14.10.2025. Agha Shahid Majeed Khan, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel seeks time to file revised questions. At his request, to come up on 21.10.2025.” On 21.10.2025, following order was passed:- “21.10.2025. Mr. Agha Shahid Majeed Khan, advocate for applicant. The questions framed for determination appear to be argumentative and agitate questions of facts. The learned counsel may file revised questions, if so instructed. To come up on 04.11.2025.” On 04.11.2025, following order was passed:- “04.11.2025. Agha Shahid Majeed Khan, advocate for applicant. Once again no question of law arising here from have been filed. As a last chance, to come up on 18.11.2025.” Even today, two questions are proposed, which are required to agitate the evidentiary burden in argumentative nature. It is noted with much concern that despite opportunities, the learned counsel for the department is unable to articulate any question of law meriting interference in the reference jurisdiction. Since no question is articulated, reference is dismissed in limine. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969.

Since no question is articulated, reference is dismissed in limine. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge ayaz
